title: Add /split skill: take an oversized bead + seam, produce N atomic child beads with dep rewiring
description:
WHY: /quality-pass and /compose's sizing audit detect oversized beads but the human has to manually re-pour formulas or edit plan.md. For "decompose into smallest atomic parts" to be mechanical rather than aspirational, splitting needs to be a one-command operation — currently the biggest friction point between /compose and /loop /build-next.

WHAT TO DO:
- Add `skills/split/SKILL.md` with frontmatter (name=split, triggers on "/split", "split this bead", "atomize bead X").
- Inputs: bead ID + optional seam description. If seam omitted, refuse with a prompt — splitting without a seam is just guessing.
- Process:
  1. Read source bead (`bd show $id --json`). Read its parent epic, dependents, dependencies.
  2. From the seam + AC, propose N child beads with: distinct titles, derived ACs, file/path ownership boundaries (basis for the filesTouched work), and a fanout dep graph (sequential = child_n+1 depends on child_n; else parallel siblings).
  3. Print the proposed split. Do NOT mutate without explicit user confirmation.
  4. On confirm: `bd create --parent <epic>` each child; `bd dep add` for child↔child and child↔external (preserve incoming/outgoing edges from the source bead); close the source bead with reason "superseded by <ids>" (verify with `bd update --help` whether a `superseded` status exists; if not, use closed + reason).
  5. Re-validate `bd dep cycles`.
- Read-only escape: if a quality-pass-style audit shows the proposed children would still be oversized (ACs>6 etc.), refuse and recommend a different seam.

Link from /quality-pass output: skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md should mention /split as the natural follow-on for beads scoring <95 on sizing penalties.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `skills/split/SKILL.md` exists with frontmatter `name: split`.
- File contains literal phrases "seam", "confirm before mutate", "bd dep add", "superseded".
- `skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md` mentions `/split` as the follow-on for oversized beads.
- README.md pipeline section names /split alongside /compose and /quality-pass.
- A junction/symlink is created in ~/.claude/skills/split (or installer task autonomous-build-t9i handles it).
close_reason: skills/split/SKILL.md created with frontmatter + required phrases; quality-pass remediation row references /split; README pipeline + repo-layout updated; symlink created in ~/.claude/skills/split; gate BOM-fix side-included

title: Add filesTouched bead metadata + conflict-aware dispatch filter in /build-batch
description:
WHY: /build-batch currently relies on the post-merge gate to catch sibling-bead file conflicts (see skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 330 — explicit concession). Every collision burns a full worker run + a gate run. We already write testPlanFile via metadata in compose Step 4d (autonomous-build-5au); extending the same mechanism to filesTouched is cheap and turns the parallelism from "hopefully non-overlapping" into actually conflict-free.

WHAT TO DO:
- Formula schema: add a `files` array under `[steps.*]` blocks in formulas/*.formula.toml (paths the step's bead may touch, including globs). Variable-substituted the same way `testPlan.file` is. Document in formulas/README.md with an example.
- compose: in Step 4d (where testPlan metadata is written), additionally parse the step's `files` array and write to bead metadata as `filesTouched` (string array). Apply variable substitution. If a step has no `files`, skip — same pattern as testPlan.
- build-batch Dispatch-Bead: read `$bead.metadata.filesTouched`. Reject the candidate if its glob-expanded set intersects any in-flight pipeline's filesTouched, OR any bead currently in $mergeQueue / $mergeInFlight. Log the skip ("[SKIP] $id deferred — would conflict with $other on $sharedPaths"). Pick the next non-conflicting candidate. If none, fall through to the normal "no free slot" state.
- Beads with no filesTouched declared: fall back to current dispatch behavior, but print a one-line warning ("[WARN] $id has no filesTouched — relying on post-merge gate; consider adding `files` to the formula step") so formula gaps surface.
- Update existing formulas (crud-feature, app-skeleton) to declare `files` per step.
- Remove the line-330 acknowledgement in build-batch/SKILL.md (or update it to describe the new behavior).

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- formulas/crud-feature.formula.toml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.toml both contain at least one `files = [...]` entry under a `[steps.*]` block.
- formulas/README.md documents the `files` field with an example.
- skills/compose/SKILL.md describes the filesTouched metadata write (in Step 4d or a new step).
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md Dispatch-Bead section includes the conflict filter — the literal phrase "filesTouched" must appear in the dispatch decision logic.
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md no longer contains the original "post-merge gate is what catches the conflict" sentence (or has been updated to describe the new filter).
close_reason: files= declared on every step in both formulas (8+6); formulas/README.md documents the field with schema + glob rules + fallback; compose Step 4d writes filesTouched alongside testPlan; build-batch Phase 1.1 filters candidates via Test-FilesTouched-Intersect, Dispatch-Bead re-checks + carries filesTouched, helper documented; do-not section line-330 rewritten; both formulas still parse via bd cook --mode=runtime

title: Emit plan.lock.json from /vision; have /compose read the lock instead of regex-parsing plan.md
description:
WHY: skills/compose/SKILL.md Step 5 (lines 107-117) regex-parses plan.md for feature names — depends on em-dash placement, bolded labels, exact markdown shape. A typo silently drops a feature, caught only by the end-of-pour coverage check. A machine-readable mirror eliminates the parse fragility without removing the human-readable plan.md.

WHAT TO DO:
- Define a JSON Schema at `schemas/plan.lock.schema.json` with the structured equivalent of plan.md:
  - `stack`: object keyed by layer (language, backend, frontend, database, orm, auth, hosting, tests, lint) → `{choice, why}`
  - `dataModel`: array of `{entity, fields, relationships, notes}`
  - `featureOrder`: array of `{name, formulas: [string], vars: object}`
  - `crossFeatureDependencies`: array of `{blocked, blocker, reason}`
  - `escalationBudget`: `{maxSessionCostUsd, maxFailuresPerTask, additionalBlockTriggers: [string]}`
  - `openQuestions`: array of `{question, blockingCompose: bool}`
  - `incomplete`: bool (true if openQuestions has any blockingCompose=true entries)

- /vision: after writing plan.md, also write `plan.lock.json` with the same content in structured form. Validate against the schema before writing. Even when openQuestions is non-empty, write the lock with `incomplete: true` so /compose can refuse cleanly with a structured reason.

- /compose: read plan.lock.json first. If `incomplete: true`, refuse with the listed open questions. If plan.lock.json missing, fall back to the current plan.md regex parse with a deprecation warning ("plan.lock.json missing — falling back to plan.md parse; rerun /vision to generate the lock"). When present, ignore the markdown for structured fields (features, vars, deps) — markdown remains the human narrative.

- Document the schema in a new `docs/PLAN_LOCK.md` and reference it from docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §"/vision".

- Update README.md pipeline diagram to show plan.md + plan.lock.json as paired outputs of /vision.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `schemas/plan.lock.schema.json` exists and is valid JSON Schema (draft 2020-12 or compatible).
- `docs/PLAN_LOCK.md` exists.
- `skills/vision/SKILL.md` Outputs section mentions plan.lock.json.
- `skills/compose/SKILL.md` Step 4 or 5 reads from plan.lock.json before plan.md.
- `README.md` pipeline section names plan.lock.json alongside plan.md.
- Backwards compatibility: a plan.md without an adjacent plan.lock.json still works (with deprecation warning).

title: Polish orchestration: explicit path staging, remote-aware pull, builder-driven completion signal
description:
Bundle of three small orchestration fixes called out during workflow review. Independent of the larger improvements; file together for cohesion.

1. **Explicit path staging in build-next Step 9.**
   skills/build-next/SKILL.md line 163 uses `git add -A`, AFTER the gate's pre-commit safety scan has already run. Anything created between the gate and the commit (kickoff receipts mishandled, scratch files, an .env an agent created to test something) is unscreened by the safety check. Replace with explicit staging derived from the kickoff's ownership boundaries — `target/jankurai/kickoff-$id.json` enumerates the files the bead may touch. Stage those paths only.

2. **Remote-aware pull in build-batch Merge-And-Close.**
   skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 245: `git pull --ff-only origin main 2>&1 | Out-Null` — the comment says "ignore if no remote configured" but the code just suppresses the error. The subsequent merge still runs against the (potentially stale) local main. Replace with:
   ```powershell
   if ((git remote) -ne $null -and (git remote) -ne '') {
     git pull --ff-only origin main
     if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { <abort merge, block bead> }
   }
   ```

3. **Builder-driven completion signal in build-batch poll loop.**
   skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 164 polls every 10s × N workers × ~30 min wall = a lot of Opus tokens spent reading idle TaskOutput. The marker is currently parsed from worker stdout. Two cheaper alternatives:
   - Workers write `<worktree>/.bd-build-complete.json` when done (same JSON content as the current BUILD_COMPLETE marker). Orchestrator watches the filesystem (PowerShell `Register-ObjectEvent` on FileSystemWatcher, or the Monitor tool with the marker path).
   - Or: have the worker `Set-Content` a sentinel and the orchestrator uses a longer sleep (60s) — less elegant but a 6× reduction without infrastructure work.
   The first option is the "real" fix; the second is the quick-win fallback if the first hits tool-availability snags.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- skills/build-next/SKILL.md Step 9 no longer contains the literal string "git add -A"; contains language about staging from kickoff ownership / explicit paths.
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md Merge-And-Close gates the pull with a `git remote` check (literal phrase "git remote" near the pull command).
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md poll loop describes a marker-file / filesystem-watch mechanism (literal phrase ".bd-build-complete" or `FileSystemWatcher`), OR documents the 60s-sleep fallback with a TODO referencing the marker-file approach.
notes: Freshness note (2026-05-28): line numbers drifted (build-next git add -A is line 162 not 163; build-batch pull is line 301 not 245; sleep is line 176 not 164) but all three constructs are unchanged and still match the bead's diagnosis.

title: Write formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml
description:
WHY: every non-trivial app needs auth, and crud-feature shoehorns are wrong shape (auth is cross-cutting, not entity-CRUD). plan.md picks for "Habits with login" currently have nowhere to land.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml` covering email + password auth:

Variables:
- `hash_strategy` (default `argon2`; alternatives `bcrypt`, `scrypt`)
- `session_strategy` (default `server-session`; alternative `jwt`)
- `session_ttl_minutes` (default `1440`)
- `require_email_verification` (default `false`)

Steps (each as a `[[steps]]` block with `files = [...]` and where applicable `[steps.testPlan]`):
1. **schema** — users + sessions tables (or sessions store keyed by session_strategy). Files: migrations/*_auth.*, models/User.*, models/Session.*.
2. **password-hashing** — password hash + verify functions per hash_strategy. Files: services/auth/passwords.*, tests/services/auth/passwords.*. testPlan: hash deterministic check, verify roundtrip, timing-safe compare.
3. **register-endpoint** — POST /auth/register. Files: routes/auth/register.*, tests/endpoints/auth/register.*. testPlan: 201 on valid, 400 on missing fields, 409 on duplicate email.
4. **login-endpoint** — POST /auth/login. Files: routes/auth/login.*, tests/endpoints/auth/login.*. testPlan: 200 + session cookie/token on valid, 401 on bad password, 401 on unknown user.
5. **session-middleware** — request gate that resolves session → user. Files: middleware/session.*, tests/middleware/session.*. testPlan: anonymous request → 401; valid session → user populated; expired → 401.
6. **logout-endpoint** — POST /auth/logout. Files: routes/auth/logout.*, tests/endpoints/auth/logout.*. testPlan: invalidates session; subsequent request → 401.

Important: every step must include a `labels = ["touches-auth"]` declaration. /build-next escalates touches-auth as a blocker (per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md) — the formula SHOULD make every auth-related step block on first encounter so the human confirms the cryptographic choices. The escalation pre-check is the design, not a bug.

Do NOT include OAuth, magic-link, MFA, or password-reset in this v1 formula. Each of those is its own follow-on formula (auth-oauth, auth-magic-link, auth-mfa, auth-password-reset). Bias toward the smallest possible "users can sign up and log in" cluster.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml` exists at repo root under formulas/.
- `bd formula list` includes `auth-email-password` (verify by grepping the output).
- `bd cook auth-email-password --mode=runtime --var hash_strategy=argon2 --var session_strategy=server-session --var session_ttl_minutes=1440 --var require_email_verification=false` exits 0 and emits a parseable JSON proto.
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares both `files = [...]` and `labels = ["touches-auth"]`.
- Steps that ship code with tests declare a `[steps.testPlan]` sub-table.
- `formulas/README.md` does not need a new section — the field schema is already documented; just keep this formula consistent with the conventions there.
close_reason: formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml created; bd formula list includes it; bd cook --mode=runtime exits 0; every step declares files= and labels=touches-auth; 5 of 6 steps declare [steps.testPlan] (schema correctly excluded matching crud-feature precedent)

title: Write formulas/page.formula.toml
description:
WHY: many features are "add a page" — a settings screen, an about page, a dashboard widget container. crud-feature is overkill (no entity); ad-hoc bd create is forbidden. Need a formula for the smallest UI unit.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/page.formula.toml` for a single named page with optional data fetch:

Variables:
- `page_name` (required, e.g. "Settings", "Dashboard")
- `route_path` (required, e.g. "/settings", "/dashboard")
- `fetches_data` (default `false`) — if true, page gets a data hook + endpoint dependency
- `data_endpoint_var` (optional) — the data endpoint to fetch from (only required when fetches_data=true)

Steps:
1. **route** — register the route in the framework's router. Files: routes/index.*, src/router.*, app/{{route_path}}/page.*. Small step, no testPlan.
2. **component** — the page component, list view, or container. Files: pages/{{page_name}}.*, components/pages/{{page_name}}.*, app/{{route_path}}/page.*.
3. **data-hook** (skipped when fetches_data=false) — fetches data from data_endpoint_var. Files: hooks/use{{page_name}}.*, src/hooks/use{{page_name}}.*. testPlan: loading state, error state, success state with data.
4. **page-test** — component-level test. Files: tests/ui/pages/{{page_name}}.*. testPlan: renders without crashing; if fetches_data: shows loading/error/data states.

Important:
- The skip-at-pour-time pattern (used in crud-feature's UI steps with `include_ui`) applies here too: if `fetches_data=false`, the data-hook step is skipped. Document the convention.
- The formula must NOT bake in a specific frontend framework — use multi-glob files lists (pages/X.* OR app/X/page.* OR src/pages/X.*) so it works for Next.js, Vite-React, SvelteKit equally.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/page.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `page`.
- `bd cook page --mode=runtime --var page_name=Settings --var route_path=/settings --var fetches_data=false` exits 0.
- `bd cook page --mode=runtime --var page_name=Dashboard --var route_path=/dashboard --var fetches_data=true --var data_endpoint_var=dashboard-summary` exits 0 AND the proto includes the data-hook step (verify by grepping the JSON).
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- The component step + page-test step both declare `[steps.testPlan]`.

title: Write formulas/migration.formula.toml
description:
WHY: standalone schema changes (add column, add index, backfill) don't fit crud-feature's "full slice" shape. Currently the only escape is reaching for bd create, which is forbidden. Many plans need one-off migrations as their own atomic unit.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/migration.formula.toml` for a single schema migration with no app-code changes:

Variables:
- `migration_name` (required, e.g. "add-archived-flag-to-habits")
- `up_outline` (required) — human-readable description of what `up` does (e.g. "ALTER TABLE habits ADD COLUMN archived BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false")
- `down_outline` (required) — what the rollback does
- `touches_existing_data` (default `false`) — if true, this migration runs against rows that exist in deployed environments

Steps:
1. **write-migration** — author the up + down migration files. Files: migrations/*_{{migration_name}}.*, db/migrations/*_{{migration_name}}.*. No testPlan — the next two steps verify it.
2. **apply-fresh** — apply the migration against a fresh DB. Files: tests/migrations/{{migration_name}}.apply.*, scripts/migrate.*. testPlan: migration runs to completion against fresh DB; schema-check confirms target state.
3. **rollback-test** — apply then revert; verify no orphaned objects. Files: tests/migrations/{{migration_name}}.rollback.*. testPlan: up then down leaves the schema identical to pre-up; column/table counts match.

Important:
- When `touches_existing_data=true`, the write-migration step MUST include a `labels = ["needs-decision"]` declaration so /build-next escalates per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md. The human must confirm the backfill strategy, locking implications, and downtime budget BEFORE the migration is written.
- Do NOT support data backfills in this v1 formula — those need their own formula (`migration-with-backfill`) because they need batched-write strategies, progress reporting, and resumability.
- The formula targets one migration. Multi-migration cluster changes (e.g. extracting a new table from a column) belong in a multi-step formula or a hand-decomposed sub-tree via /split.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/migration.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `migration`.
- `bd cook migration --mode=runtime --var migration_name=add-archived-flag-to-habits --var up_outline="ALTER TABLE habits ADD COLUMN archived BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false" --var down_outline="ALTER TABLE habits DROP COLUMN archived" --var touches_existing_data=false` exits 0.
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- apply-fresh + rollback-test declare `[steps.testPlan]`.
- When poured with `touches_existing_data=true`, the resulting write-migration step has the `needs-decision` label (verify by grepping the proto JSON).

title: Write formulas/background-job.formula.toml
description:
WHY: scheduled cleanups, queue-driven webhooks, batched recomputes — background jobs are common but have no formula. crud-feature has no concept of "non-request-driven work." Without a formula, planners default to "we'll figure it out later," which means /build-next never builds it.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/background-job.formula.toml` for a single scheduled or queue-triggered job:

Variables:
- `job_name` (required, e.g. "daily-habit-rollup", "process-webhook")
- `trigger_type` (required: `schedule` | `queue`)
- `schedule` (only required when trigger_type=schedule; cron expression or interval, e.g. "0 3 * * *")
- `queue_name` (only required when trigger_type=queue)
- `idempotent` (default `true`) — declares whether the job tolerates re-runs; the formula uses this to decide whether to scaffold an idempotency key check

Steps:
1. **handler** — the job function itself. Files: jobs/{{job_name}}.*, src/jobs/{{job_name}}.*.
2. **trigger-wiring** — register the job with the scheduler (cron config, schedule registration) OR the queue subscription. Files: config/jobs.*, src/scheduler.*, src/workers/{{job_name}}.*. No testPlan — wiring is verified by integration test in the next step.
3. **idempotency-guard** (skipped when idempotent=true, i.e. when the handler is naturally idempotent and needs no key check) — adds a per-invocation key check. Files: jobs/{{job_name}}.guard.*, services/idempotency.*. testPlan: duplicate invocation returns early; first invocation runs.
4. **handler-test** — unit test for the handler function. Files: tests/jobs/{{job_name}}.*. testPlan: handler runs to completion on representative input; handler is observably idempotent under repeated invocation if idempotent=true; on failure handler emits the expected error signal (re-raise for queue retry, log + skip for cron).

Important:
- This formula targets one job. Multi-stage pipelines (e.g. extract → transform → load workflows) need a different formula or a hand-decomposed sub-tree.
- Do NOT scaffold a worker process here. If the app doesn't have a worker, that's a separate `app-skeleton`-level concern.
- The handler-test must hit the handler directly with a fake input, not go through the trigger. Trigger-side integration tests (cron firing, queue draining) are out of scope for v1.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/background-job.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `background-job`.
- `bd cook background-job --mode=runtime --var job_name=daily-habit-rollup --var trigger_type=schedule --var schedule="0 3 * * *" --var idempotent=true` exits 0.
- `bd cook background-job --mode=runtime --var job_name=process-webhook --var trigger_type=queue --var queue_name=webhooks --var idempotent=false` exits 0 AND the proto INCLUDES the idempotency-guard step (verify by grepping).
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- handler + handler-test + idempotency-guard (when present) declare `[steps.testPlan]`.

title: Write formulas/integration-http.formula.toml
description:
WHY: every app eventually integrates with an outbound HTTP API (payment, email, third-party SaaS). Currently no formula covers this; the secret-handling step would touch escalation rules. A formula that structures the work so non-secret pieces can ship in parallel while secret handling waits for human input is exactly the "smallest atomic parts + orchestrate" goal.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/integration-http.formula.toml` for an outbound HTTP client integration:

Variables:
- `integration_name` (required, e.g. "stripe", "sendgrid", "internal-billing")
- `base_url` (required)
- `auth_strategy` (required: `api-key` | `oauth-client-credentials` | `hmac-signed` | `none`)
- `retry_policy` (default `"exponential-3-attempts"`; alternatives `"linear-5-attempts"`, `"none"`)
- `timeout_seconds` (default `30`)

Steps:
1. **client** — the HTTP client class/module with retry policy applied. No secret handling here yet — accepts credentials as a constructor/init parameter. Files: services/integrations/{{integration_name}}/client.*, src/integrations/{{integration_name}}/client.*. testPlan: client constructs with sample creds; client honors retry_policy on 5xx; client respects timeout_seconds.
2. **secret-handling** — wires credentials from env/secret store into client construction. Files: config/secrets/{{integration_name}}.*, services/secrets.*. **MUST declare `labels = ["touches-secrets"]`** so /build-next escalates per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md. The human decides the secret store, rotation policy, and access-control before this step runs.
3. **happy-path-test** — integration test against a mocked HTTP server or replay fixtures. Files: tests/integrations/{{integration_name}}/happy.*. testPlan: representative successful call returns parsed response; client makes the expected requests in the expected order.
4. **error-path-test** — integration test for retry behavior + error surfaces. Files: tests/integrations/{{integration_name}}/errors.*. testPlan: 5xx triggers retry per policy; 4xx surfaces typed error; timeout surfaces typed error; final failure after retries surfaces typed error.

Important:
- Steps 1, 3, 4 are independent of secret-handling and SHIP IN PARALLEL with secret-handling waiting. The formula's needs= declarations should NOT make step 1 depend on step 2 — only `secret-handling` blocks on human input.
- Do NOT mock secret handling in tests. Tests construct the client with hardcoded fake credentials directly; secret handling is a separate plumbing concern verified once by /build-next's gate (or manually after the human confirms).
- Do NOT add request signing, OAuth refresh flows, or rate-limit handling in v1. Each is its own follow-on formula (`integration-http-oauth-refresh`, `integration-http-rate-limited`).

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/integration-http.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `integration-http`.
- `bd cook integration-http --mode=runtime --var integration_name=stripe --var base_url=https://api.stripe.com --var auth_strategy=api-key --var retry_policy=exponential-3-attempts --var timeout_seconds=30` exits 0.
- secret-handling step declares `labels = ["touches-secrets"]` (verify by grepping the proto JSON for `touches-secrets`).
- Step `needs:` graph allows client / happy-path-test / error-path-test to run independently of secret-handling (verify by inspecting `needs` arrays — none of these three should list `secret-handling` as a need).
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- client + happy-path-test + error-path-test declare `[steps.testPlan]`.

title: /build-next: read app tenets.md before making judgment calls
description: Now that /vision produces a per-app tenets.md (autonomous-build-yxn), the builder needs to actually consult it. Update skills/build-next/SKILL.md so the builder reads tenets.md as part of its context-kickoff phase, before any judgment-call moment. Suggested: a small 'Tenets check' step in the build-next process that names tenets.md alongside the bead spec as inputs to the implementation phase, plus a note in the 'Do not' section that tenet violations should escalate, not be improvised around. Acceptance: skills/build-next/SKILL.md mentions tenets.md as a context input; the workflow-level docs/TENETS.md is referenced as the fallback when the per-app file doesn't cover a question.

title: Create beads-builder subagent in .claude/agents/
description: The worker side of /build-batch. Wraps build-next Steps 3-9 (read issue, escalation pre-check, jankurai kickoff, implement, run post-build-gate, commit on bead/$id) but does NOT do worktree create/remove (orchestrator does) or close (orchestrator does after successful merge). Emits a structured <!-- BUILD_COMPLETE:{beadId,status,commitSha} --> marker on stdout. Status values: closed-ready-to-merge | blocked | failed. New file: .claude/agents/beads-builder.md.
close_reason: Created .claude/agents/beads-builder.md with input/output contracts, 8-step process, and explicit non-responsibilities (no claim/close/worktree-mgmt/merge). Subagent emits a single BUILD_COMPLETE marker the orchestrator parses.

title: smbuild /decompose run 2 — repro findings (2026-05-28)
description: Reproduction report from the SECOND /decompose run on smbuild, after run-1's workflow fixes (epic autonomous-build-cnv) landed and the plan's 5 integration auth bindings were patched. Those fixes made the run truthful (quality phase now scores; pour agents now fail honestly instead of improvising), which exposed a deeper, previously-masked plan defect. Full report: retros/repro-smbuild-decompose-run2-2026-05-28.md. These are repro reports for investigation, not prescribed solutions.

title: /vision picks generic formulas, not stack-native variants, for a Rust/gRPC app
description:
REPRO (smbuild /decompose run 2, 2026-05-28):
1. docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md pins the stack to Rust (core/services = Rust; contracts = gRPC/tonic/ConnectRPC per plan).
2. /vision produced plan.lock.json that bound the GENERIC formulas to Rust backend features: Repo skeleton, Closed grammar, OpenTelemetry, Terraform -> app-skeleton; Tenant boot, Audit chain -> background-job; Customer/Property/Job/Inventory/Invoice -> crud-feature; ConnectRPC contracts -> app-skeleton; Zitadel/OpenFGA -> integration-http.
3. /decompose pours these. RESULT: 7 of 21 features fail to pour because the generic formula vars cannot express a Rust stack:
   - app-skeleton requires package_manager in {npm,pnpm,uv,poetry,auto}; plan supplied cargo / terraform / n-a -> off-enum, pour aborts (Repo skeleton, Closed grammar, OpenTelemetry, Terraform).
   - background-job requires trigger_type in {schedule,queue}; plan supplied internal / cron -> off-enum (Tenant boot, Audit chain).
   - migration requires down_outline; plan omitted it (Universal schema migration).
4. The 14 that DID pour carry the same root cause into their beads: crud-feature is REST-shaped, so Customer/Property/etc. beads specify REST verbs + HTTP status codes, contradicting the locked gRPC/tonic stack (see quality penalties on smbuild-mol-3ww and siblings). Zitadel/OpenFGA used integration-http even though the plan itself annotated them [needs-formula: oidc-client-rust] and [needs-formula: openfga-model].

EXPECTED: for a Rust-locked stack, /vision should select the stack-native variants that already exist in the library: app-skeleton-rust-cargo, crud-feature-rust, tenant-boot-rust, audit-chain-rust, otel-bootstrap-rust, oidc-client-rust, openfga-model, composer-grammar-version, grpc-tonic-service, terraform-aws-baseline. Those native formulas declare Rust-appropriate vars (no package_manager/trigger_type enum mismatch) and emit gRPC, not REST.

ACTUAL: /vision defaulted to the generic JS/Python/REST formulas. This was invisible in run 1 because pour agents silently improvised vars and the quality phase was a no-op; run-1 fixes (autonomous-build-l6g, -49u) exposed it.

This is distinct from autonomous-build-bje (which validates a var NAME against a chosen formula). Here the wrong FORMULA was chosen in the first place.
acceptance_criteria: On a Rust-locked stack, /vision selects the -rust / native formula variants (app-skeleton-rust-cargo, crud-feature-rust, tenant-boot-rust, audit-chain-rust, otel-bootstrap-rust, oidc-client-rust, openfga-model, composer-grammar-version, grpc-tonic-service, terraform-aws-baseline) rather than the generic ones; a fresh /decompose then pours every such feature with zero off-enum package_manager/trigger_type failures and emits gRPC (not REST) CRUD beads.

title: migration and secret-store formulas reference docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md that does not exist in app repos
description: REPRO (smbuild /decompose run 2, 2026-05-28): multiple poured beads from the migration formula and the secret-store steps cite docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md, but that file does not exist in a freshly-initialized app repo (it lives, if anywhere, in autonomous-build). Quality scoring penalized these beads for a broken/dependency pointer: e.g. smbuild-mol-val (Customer schema migration), smbuild-mol-j9k (Invoice migration), smbuild-mol-b5e (Inventory migration), smbuild-mol-atv (Job migration), and the wire-credentials beads (smbuild-mol-7se, -uew, -djf, -451). EXPECTED: a formula should not point app beads at a meta-repo file absent from the app; either drop the pointer, inline the rule, or have init create the file. ACTUAL: dead pointer in every migration/secret bead.
acceptance_criteria: Pouring a migration or secret-store bead into a fresh app repo yields no reference to a nonexistent docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md (the formula omits/inlines the rule, or repo init creates the file).

title: cross-dep wiring applied 0 of 28 edges, including edges between two successfully-poured features
description: REPRO (smbuild /decompose run 2, 2026-05-28): decomposeReport.md reports 'Cross-feature deps applied: 0' and the fidelity verifier found only intra-molecule edges. Some missing edges touch the 7 unpoured features (expected to fail), BUT several declared edges are between TWO features that both poured successfully and still were not wired, e.g. Customer CRUD (smbuild-mol-cci) <- ConnectRPC/gRPC contracts v1 (smbuild-mol-jlk); Property CRUD <- Customer CRUD; Job <- Customer/Property. Those should have been wireable. This suggests the Phase-3 cross-dep wiring still under-applies (feature-name -> molecule-epic-id resolution failing, or the wiring aborting when ANY endpoint is unresolvable). NOTE: autonomous-build-zip (verify edges landed) is closed; this indicates that fix is incomplete or a separate resolution bug remains. Needs confirmation by the maintainer. EXPECTED: edges between two poured features are applied and counted.
acceptance_criteria: A /decompose run where two dependent features both pour successfully wires their declared cross-feature edge (it appears in bd dep), and the report applied-count is greater than 0 whenever poured-to-poured edges exist.

title: Provide a cross-platform installer (install.sh); install.ps1 is Windows-only
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: install.ps1 already hard-links formulas/ into ~/.beads/formulas/ and workflows/ into ~/.claude/workflows/, but it is PowerShell and only runs on Windows. On this Linux host it never ran, so ~/.beads/formulas/ was empty and /decompose pre-flight could not resolve the plan formulas (app-skeleton, migration, background-job, crud-feature, page, integration-http). Workaround used: symlinked formulas into ~/.beads/formulas/ by hand. Verification: confirmed bd search paths are .beads/formulas, ~/.beads/formulas, GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas, and none pointed at autonomous-build/formulas.
acceptance_criteria: An executable install.sh exists at repo root that symlinks formulas/*.toml into ~/.beads/formulas/ and workflows/*.js into ~/.claude/workflows/, mirroring install.ps1; README documents it for non-Windows hosts.
close_reason: Added executable install.sh (POSIX counterpart to install.ps1): symlinks skills/, formulas/*, and workflows/*.js into ~/.claude and ~/.beads; --dry-run/--force flags; stale-skill cleanup. README documents per-platform installer choice.

title: decompose quality phase scored zero beads (silent no-op, gate passes vacuously)
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: log line 'Quality scoring done: 0 epics scored, 0 beads below 95' despite 18 successful pours and ~111 child beads. Phase 5 epic-discovery returned no epics with open non-epic children, so the per-epic scoring fanned out to nothing and the all-beads-at-95 verdict condition passed vacuously - no bead was actually scored against the buildability rubric. Likely the parent relationship is app-epic to molecule-root-epic to children, and epic-discovery did not traverse to the molecule-root epics. Verification: workflow logs plus live bd list showing 21 open epics and non-epic children present.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/decompose.js Phase 5 epic-discovery enumerates molecule-root epics (children of the app epic) so a non-empty pour set yields at least one epic and at least one scored bead; synthesis treats an empty quality-score set as NEEDS-FIX rather than a vacuous pass.
close_reason: Phase 5 epic-discovery now enumerates every open epic directly parenting a non-epic bead (molecule-root + sub-epics) and returns exact totalOpenNonEpic ground truth via schema. Synthesis adds qualityVacuous/qualityUndercovered guards: a zero-scored or under-scored run forces NEEDS-FIX instead of vacuously passing every(). Spec Phase 5 + Phase 8 updated.

title: /build-next: support meta-work mode (no Jankurai when no AGENTS.md)
description: /build-next assumes the repo is a Jankurai-tracked app scaffolded by /compose (has AGENTS.md, agent/baselines/, target/jankurai/ gitignored). When run on autonomous-build itself (meta-work editing skills/ and formulas/), none of that exists — Step 6 (jankurai kickoff) would refuse, Step 8's jankurai pass in post-build-gate is irrelevant. WHAT TO DO: add a meta-mode detection at the top of skills/build-next/SKILL.md — if AGENTS.md is absent at repo root, switch to meta-mode: (a) skip Step 5 worktree creation OR keep it (decide based on whether bd worktree still makes sense for skill/formula edits — probably yes for isolation), (b) skip Step 6 jankurai kickoff entirely, (c) in Step 8 run only the non-jankurai parts of post-build-gate.ps1, (d) commit + close steps unchanged. Document the mode switch in the skill. Caught while trying to /build-next the workflow-improvements epic, 2026-05-28.

title: /build-batch: post-merge gate on main as defense-in-depth
description: The per-worker quality gate runs in the worker's isolated worktree. That catches isolated regressions but not "passed in isolation, broken when combined with sibling bead." Add a post-merge gate step inside the orchestrator's serialized merge: after `git merge --no-ff bead/$id` lands on main, re-run hooks/post-build-gate.ps1 on main. If it fails, `git reset --hard ORIG_HEAD` to undo the merge, mark the bead blocked with the failure summary, do NOT close the bead. This is the safety net for the (currently) no-testPlanFile world.
close_reason: Scope delivered as part of g6s: Merge-And-Close runs hooks/post-build-gate.ps1 on main after every merge and git reset --hard HEAD~1 + bd update --status=blocked on failure. Implemented in skills/build-batch/SKILL.md (Serialized merge step section).

title: Add freshness check to /build-next before claiming a bead
description: Borrowed from refs/epic-quality-pass §Freshness check. A bead is a snapshot of the codebase at filing time. Other beads (or human work) may have landed since and silently satisfied this bead's AC. Today /build-next just claims the next ready bead and dispatches a builder — even if the work is already done. WHAT TO DO: in skills/build-next/SKILL.md, before calling bd update --status=in_progress, run a quick freshness check on the bead's load-bearing claims. If the bead says a file should not exist, glob to confirm. If it asserts wiring is missing, grep to confirm the wiring still isn't there. If it cites file sizes or line counts, spot-check. Three outcomes: (a) fresh → proceed normally; (b) drifted (details moved but gap still real) → proceed but record a 'freshness note' in the builder's prompt; (c) stale (AC already met) → close the bead with reason 'AC already met by <ref>' and move to the next ready bead. Keep the check fast — a few grep/glob calls, not a deep code review.

title: /compose: parse [steps.testPlan] and write bd metadata after pour
description: Formulas now declare [steps.testPlan] blocks (autonomous-build-a2m) with file, cases, coverage fields. bd 0.55.3 silently drops these from the cooked proto, so the spawned beads have no testPlan metadata. The /quality-pass skill (autonomous-build-74i) expects metadata.testPlanFile to be populated, so compose must write it. WHAT TO DO: in skills/compose/SKILL.md, after the bd mol pour for each formula, (1) read the formula TOML directly from formulas/<name>.formula.toml, (2) extract each step's [steps.testPlan] block (with var substitution applied), (3) match each step.title (after var substitution) against the spawned issue.title from bd show <pour-root-id> --json (dependents[].title is unique within one pour), (4) for each match with a testPlan block, write bd metadata: bd update <issue-id> --metadata @<tempfile.json> where the json contains testPlanFile, testPlanCases, testPlanCoverage. Use the @file pattern because coverage strings contain semicolons that break inline JSON. This bead is the consumer for the testPlan convention added in a2m.

title: Add plan.md coverage check to /compose: every feature must produce >=1 epic
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads coverage matrix idea (stripped down). Today /compose walks plan.md §'Feature order' and pours formulas, but never verifies that every feature line in plan.md actually produced at least one epic in the DAG. A misnamed formula or a typo in plan.md silently drops features. WHAT TO DO: after pour completes in skills/compose/SKILL.md, parse plan.md §'Feature order' to extract the feature names, then diff against the titles of newly-created epics under the app's top-level epic. Report any feature name that didn't produce an epic. Same disposition as the sizing audit (sibling bead): surface in the compose summary, don't block. Skip the full word-count density matrix from the refs skill — that's only useful for freeform→beads conversion, not formula pours.

title: Add sizing audit to /compose: flag oversized beads after pour
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads sizing rubric. Builder agents have a ~70-85K token effective budget after fixed overhead. A formula step like crud-feature.endpoints already implies 5+ files and 6 ACs in one bead — at the upper bound of what the builder can finish. WHAT TO DO: after the formula pours complete in skills/compose/SKILL.md, walk every spawned bead and apply a lightweight sizing check: count ACs, count files mentioned in description, detect cross-layer language (UI + API + DB in one bead). Flag beads exceeding thresholds (>6 ACs, >5 files, multi-layer scope) in the compose summary printed to the user. Do NOT auto-split or block — surface, let the user decide. This is intentionally narrower than the design-to-beads rubric (no penalty math, no scoring) because formula-poured beads are usually well-shaped; the audit just catches outliers.

title: Build /quality-pass skill: read-only audit of an epic before /loop /build-next
description: Borrowed from refs/epic-quality-pass. Today /compose validates structurally only (no cycles, has-ready). Sized-wrong or vaguely-spec'd beads make it to /build-next and waste a worktree before failing. WHAT TO DO: create skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md as a read-only skill that takes an epic ID, scores each child bead 0-100 against a penalty rubric (sizing: files/ACs/cross-layer reach; spec concreteness: vague ACs, missing file paths, missing API contracts; context: missing links, undefined domain terms; risk: new external integrations), and proposes specific remediations to reach >=95. Include the freshness check (verify the bead's load-bearing claims still hold against current code) before applying the rubric — stale beads get recommended for closure, not remediation. Output a markdown report; never call mutating bd commands. Hook it so /compose suggests running it before /loop /build-next. The skill must assert that metadata.testPlanFile is populated on each bead — depends on sibling formula-metadata work landing first.

title: Add testPlan{File,Cases,Coverage} fields to formula schema and existing formulas
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads §Test Architecture. Every formula step today implies tests (endpoint-tests, ui-tests) but writes no per-bead test metadata. /build-next has no signal for where tests live, so each bead invents a new test file — at scale this fragments coverage. WHAT TO DO: (1) extend formula step schema with optional testPlanFile (string path), testPlanCases (int), testPlanCoverage (semicolon-separated string). (2) extend bd cook / bd mol pour so these flow into bd metadata at pour time. (3) retrofit formulas/crud-feature.formula.yaml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.yaml — for each test-flavored step, set the three fields with sensible defaults parameterized by the step's variables. (4) document in formulas/README.md. This unblocks the quality-pass skill (sibling bead) which will assert metadata.testPlanFile is populated.
notes:
FOUNDATIONAL BLOCKER DISCOVERED 2026-05-28:

While starting work on testPlan{File,Cases,Coverage} fields, I verified the existing formulas don't actually load into bd 0.55.3.

What I tested:
- `bd formula list` from autonomous-build repo → "No formulas found" despite .formula.yaml files being in the search path.
- Copied crud-feature.formula.yaml to a fresh bd-init'd project's .beads/formulas/ → still "No formulas found".
- bd formula convert only handles JSON → TOML; no YAML support.
- Wrote a tiny JSON test formula → bd parsed it but dropped the `name:` field (canonical field is `formula = "..."`), dropped `variables`, dropped `acceptance` per step.

Comparing autonomous-build YAML vs bd's canonical schema (see beads-release.formula.toml):
- `name:` should be `formula = "..."` (top-level)
- `variables: [...]` should be `[vars.<name>]` tables
- `depends_on:` per step should be `needs:`
- `acceptance:` per step has no bd equivalent — must be embedded in description
- `priority:` and `labels:` per step are not in the canonical example
- bd requires top-level `type = "workflow"` and `version = 1`

Implication: the entire /compose pipeline is non-functional against bd 0.55.3 today. `bd cook crud-feature` fails because the formula isn't recognized. Adding testPlan fields to YAML formulas bd can't load is wasted motion.

Right sequence:
1. Convert formulas to bd's canonical TOML schema (foundational — new bead)
2. Validate the converted formulas end-to-end with bd cook + bd mol pour against a scratch project (foundational — new bead)
3. THEN add testPlan fields to the working TOML formulas (this bead, autonomous-build-a2m)
4. Update /compose to write the testPlan blocks as bd metadata after pour

Blocking this bead until 1 and 2 are filed and closed.

title: plan-stage coverage gate: must-haves + concerns
description:
Guarantee every vision.md must-have and every success-metric step makes it into plan.lock.json, with a falsifiable coverage map that blocks /decompose when something is dropped. Closes the gap where /vision can silently drop a must-have during freeform feature decomposition: every downstream gate validates against the plan, never against the vision, so a dropped must-have is invisible until ship. MVP = bfo.1-4. Follow-up = bfo.5.

EXPANDED 2026-05-28 (design session): folds in cross-cutting CONCERN coverage (data-model, authn, authz, secrets, data-lifecycle, error-handling, observability, external-integrations, perf-envelope, abuse-surface) on the SAME mechanism. A concern is forced to a decided state - addressed (with falsifiable evidence) or excluded (with reason) - never silently omitted. This attacks OMISSION (invisible) where the must-have track attacks dropped product features.

Concern track: vocabulary (bfo.6) -> schema concerns[] (folded into bfo.1) -> vision emit (bfo.7) -> decidedness + required-excluded contradiction gate (bfo.8) -> decompose truthfulness verify (bfo.9). The decidedness gate reuses the same incomplete:true -> /decompose-refusal plumbing as the must-have gate (bfo.4); no new downstream gate for the MVP. Only the truthfulness check (bfo.9, "addressed" actually traces to a bead) is genuinely new decompose-side work, and it is follow-up grade like bfo.5.

The larger /vision -> dynamic-workflow conversion is a SEPARATE epic that DEPENDS on this contract. Land this against the current skill first; do not couple coverage value to the rewrite.

title: plan.lock schema v2: add mustHaves[], successMetric, coverage[], concerns[]
description:
Add FOUR fields to schemas/plan.lock.schema.json and bump schemaVersion const 1->2.

WHY: the lock currently has no representation of must-haves, the success metric, or cross-cutting concerns (security/data/infra/...). You cannot check coverage of a set the contract never records. additionalProperties:false (line 18) rejects new fields until added. concerns[] is folded into THIS bump (one schema version, not two) per the 2026-05-28 design session.

ACCEPTANCE:
- schemaVersion const becomes 2.
- New required field mustHaves: array of {id (string, e.g. M1), text (string, minLength 1)}.
- New required field successMetric: object {steps: array of {id (string, e.g. S1), text (string)}}.
- New required field coverage: array of {mustHaveId (string), features (array of strings, minItems 1), how (string, minLength 1)}.
- New required field concerns: array of {concernId (string), status (enum "addressed"|"excluded"), evidence (string), reason (string)}. evidence required when status=addressed; reason required when status=excluded (if/then or oneOf). concernId is a free string validated against docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md by /vision, NOT enumerated in the schema - so vocabulary edits never force a schema bump.
- additionalProperties:false preserved on every new object.
- docs/PLAN_LOCK.md updated to document all four new fields.
- Verify: a hand-written v2 lock validates; a v1-shaped lock fails on the schemaVersion const; a concerns[] entry with status=addressed but no evidence fails validation.
filesTouched: schemas/plan.lock.schema.json, docs/PLAN_LOCK.md

title: decompose: accept plan.lock schemaVersion 2
description:
Update the Phase 1 version check so /decompose accepts schemaVersion 2.

WHY: decompose.spec.md:46 hard-fails on any schemaVersion != 1. The T1 bump to 2 would make every /decompose run refuse unless this is updated in lockstep (T3 sync rule: spec + js same commit).

ACCEPTANCE:
- workflows/decompose.spec.md Phase 1 step 3 accepts schemaVersion 2 (and states whether 1 is still tolerated or rejected with a migration message — recommend: reject 1 with 'rerun /vision to regenerate a v2 lock').
- workflows/decompose.js version-check matches the spec exactly (edited in the SAME commit per the spec's sync rule, decompose.spec.md:14-16,400).
- Verify: a v2 lock passes preflight; a v1 lock fails with the migration message.
filesTouched: workflows/decompose.spec.md, workflows/decompose.js

title: vision: ID must-haves + success-metric steps; emit coverage map + plan.md table
description:
Make /vision populate the new v2 fields and surface them to the human.

WHY: must-haves live only as freeform checkboxes in vision.md S3 and success metric as prose in S8; they evaporate when /vision writes the plan. Step 1 quotes back non-goals/constraints/success-metric but NOT must-haves (skills/vision/SKILL.md:18).

ACCEPTANCE:
- /vision assigns stable IDs to each vision.md S3 must-have (M1, M2, ...) and decomposes the S8 success metric into observable steps (S1, S2, ...).
- Writes mustHaves[], successMetric{steps[]}, and coverage[] (mustHaveId -> {features, how}) into plan.lock.json.
- coverage[].how states HOW the feature delivers the must-have (evidence, not a bare link) - mirror of decompose anti-vagueness invariant (decompose.spec.md:185).
- plan.md gains a '## Coverage' section: a must-have -> feature traceability table mirroring the lock map.
- SKILL.md step 1 quote-back enumerates the IDed must-haves; closing /vision summary prints the coverage table.
- Verify: run /vision on a sample vision.md; lock has all three fields; plan.md has Coverage table; summary lists must-haves.
filesTouched: skills/vision/SKILL.md, templates/vision.md

title: vision: forward-coverage assertion blocks /decompose on a dropped must-have
description:
The actual check. Assert every must-have is covered; block downstream if not.

WHY: this is the gate. Without it the coverage map from bfo.3 is documentation, not enforcement.

ACCEPTANCE:
- /vision asserts every mustHaves[].id appears in coverage[] with >=1 feature (forward coverage).
- Any uncovered must-have becomes an openQuestions[] entry with blockingCompose:true, which flips incomplete:true (schema lines 181-184).
- Reuses existing plumbing: /decompose Phase 1 already refuses when incomplete==true (decompose.spec.md:47) - no new downstream gate needed.
- The /vision summary states PASS (all must-haves covered) or lists the uncovered must-haves.
- Verify: a vision.md with a must-have mapping to no feature produces incomplete:true + a blocking openQuestion, and /decompose refuses with that reason.
filesTouched: skills/vision/SKILL.md

title: vision: reverse-trace (no orphan features) + success-metric step oracle
description:
Follow-up hardening beyond MVP. Not required to close the dropped-must-have hole.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Reverse trace: every featureOrder[] entry traces to >=1 must-have OR nice-to-have; orphans surface as plan-time scope drift (vision-level analog of decompose Phase 6.B traceability).
- Success-metric oracle: every successMetric.steps[] entry maps to >=1 covered feature; an unsatisfiable step blocks (catches must-haves individually present but not composing into the working flow).
- Both failures route through openQuestions blockingCompose:true (same wiring as bfo.4).
- Verify: a plan with an orphan feature and a plan with an unsatisfiable success-metric step each block.
filesTouched: skills/vision/SKILL.md

title: concerns: pin docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md vocabulary (addressed-means + default applicability + derivation)
description:
Pin the concern vocabulary the plan-stage coverage gate checks against. The DEFAULT_STACK.md of coverage.

WHY: /vision can silently omit security/data/infra concerns today - nothing forces the plan to even mention authz or secrets. A pinned vocabulary + a falsifiable "addressed means..." definition per concern is what makes omission detectable. Downstream vision-workflow agents run in the app cwd and cannot read this doc, so the vocabulary will ALSO be inlined as a JS const there (kept in sync, decompose.spec.md<->decompose.js pattern) - but this file is the human source of truth.

- New file docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md.
- Core concern vocabulary (each a stable concernId): data-model, authn, authz, secrets, data-lifecycle, error-handling, observability, external-integrations, perf-envelope, abuse-surface.
- Each concern has: id, one-line meaning, and an "addressed means..." column stating what counts as concrete evidence (e.g. authn -> "who authenticates + mechanism, cited to a feature/formula", NOT "we handle auth").
- Each concern has a default applicability (required | optional | excluded-by-default) AND the rule that DERIVES it from vision.md: abuse-surface excluded unless a public network surface; secrets+data-lifecycle required if S6 has a privacy/budget constraint; authn+authz required if any must-have implies user accounts; perf-envelope required if S8 success metric implies scale/latency.
- Documents what is NOT a concern here and why: testing (pinned by DEFAULT_STACK + scored per-bead in decompose Phase 5), hosting/infra (DEFAULT_STACK: chosen at deploy time -> ships as a pinned default exclusion). accessibility/i18n deferred to future per-archetype profiles, NOT core.
- States the decided-state rule: every applicable concern is addressed | excluded, never undecided; required+excluded is a contradiction that stops for the human.

title: vision -> dynamic workflow (hybrid: thin skill shell + concern fan-out engine)
description:
Convert /vision from a turn-by-turn skill into a dynamic workflow (like /decompose and /build-batch), keeping a THIN /vision skill as the human-facing conversational shell. Decided in the 2026-05-28 design session.

SHAPE (hybrid): the thin skill does the product dialog (reads vision.md, quotes back non-goals/constraints/success-metric/must-haves, helps fill gaps), then invokes the vision workflow; the workflow is the deterministic engine. Skill warmth at the edges, deterministic engine in the middle.

WHY a workflow: per-concern purpose-built agents (1 per applicable concern) with structured outputs, a COMPUTED decidedness verdict (not vibes-in-chat), state isolation, and the adversarial-verification machinery - exactly the determinism properties the design session was chasing. A skill spawning agents ad hoc (as /vision's 3-agent off-stack consult already does) gives none of those guarantees. The human-in-loop tension is resolved by the thin shell + a NEEDS-INPUT return that maps to vision.md edits + re-run (a durable-input loop, more auditable than transient chat Q&A).

PHASE STRUCTURE:
1. intake/validate vision.md (sequential) -> NEEDS-INPUT blocking questions if incoherent/incomplete.
2. skeleton: data model + feature order + formula picks (sequential; these are coupled).
3. concern fan-out: 1 agent per applicable concern, each reading the FROZEN skeleton read-only, deciding only its concern, emitting {status, evidence|reason}. (Frozen-skeleton input is load-bearing: concerns are far more coupled than features, so fanning out from scratch yields N inconsistent mental models.)
4. reconcile: contradiction scan (required+excluded), assemble plan.lock v2 + tenets + plan.md, compute decidedness verdict -> COMPLETE | NEEDS-INPUT.

DEPENDS ON the coverage-gate contract (epic autonomous-build-bfo): PLAN_CONCERNS.md vocabulary, plan.lock v2 concerns[], and the derivation rules must exist first. This is the LATER, larger track. Children may be atomized further at build time.

ACCEPTANCE:
- New file workflows/vision.spec.md.
- Documents the hybrid model (thin /vision skill shell + workflow engine) and why vision stays human-in-the-loop while the engine is deterministic.
- Specifies all 4 phases (intake, skeleton, concern fan-out, reconcile) with per-agent tools, inputs, outputs, and structured-output schemas.
- States the self-contained-prompt rule (agents run in app cwd; spec does not ship) and the concern-vocabulary-inlined-as-JS-const sync rule (PLAN_CONCERNS.md <-> vision.js, mirroring decompose.spec.md<->decompose.js).
- Specifies the COMPLETE | NEEDS-INPUT return contract and how NEEDS-INPUT maps to vision.md edits + re-run.
- Cross-references the bfo contract (concerns[], coverage[], PLAN_CONCERNS.md).

description: ESCALATION_RULES.md:15 - one retry biases surviving output toward easy beads, strands hard/load-bearing ones. Fix: reconsider retry budget / escalation policy for load-bearing beads.

title: orchestrator: no end-to-end driver for vision->decompose->build-batch->retro
description: Nothing sequences the four stages; /loop only iterates /build-next within the build stage (README.md:20, ARCHITECTURE.md:43). Human is the orchestrator at every seam except build->retro. Fix: top-level build-app driver that takes a filled vision.md and sequences all four stages with checkpoints it can clear autonomously.

title: build-batch: merge conflicts permanently block with no rebase/retry
description: build-batch.js:414-421 - shared config/barrel/lockfiles accumulate over the run, so conflict-blocks rise exactly when the human is furthest away. Fix: auto-rebase + re-run gate before blocking on merge conflict.

title: retro: spec exists but no retro.js/skill; build-next auto-invokes nothing

description: build-next/SKILL.md still cites the removed /compose stage; build-batch.js agent prompts still reference build-batch.spec.md (the bug already fixed in decompose.js). Fix: scrub stale references.

title: vision: front-load auth/secrets/migration decisions so touches-auth beads clear unattended

A login-bearing app blocks mid-run and pages an absent human. Fix: capture auth-model + secrets choices at human-present /vision time into plan.lock so labeled beads clear unattended.

title: build-batch: no auto-resume after a block (loop exits, only human restart)
description: /escalate sends PushNotification and the loop exits; only restart is a human typing /loop /build-next (escalate/SKILL.md:38,52, ESCALATION_RULES.md:40-41). After drain-to-blocked nothing runs for the rest of the 2 days. Fix: polling driver that re-checks bd ready on interval and resumes when unblocked (or budget/timeout fires).

title: build-batch: --budget parsed but never enforced
description: Flag appears only at build-batch.js:28,34, never read in the 519-line wave loop. The one financial safety stop for an unattended run does not exist. Fix: wire cumulative-cost accounting into the dispatch loop per build-batch.spec.md:425.

title: build-batch: hung worker stalls the whole batch forever (no per-worker timeout)
description: build-batch.js:362 await parallel(buildTasks) has no timeout; spec's 30-min stageTimeout/TaskStop (build-batch.spec.md:411-415) never implemented. A wedged worker never returns so parallel() never resolves: silent infinite stall, no notification. Fix: wrap each worker thunk in the spec'd per-worker timeout.

title: build-batch: crash mid-batch is unrecoverable (all state in-memory)
description: Declared a non-goal (build-batch.spec.md:101,440); over a 2-day window an OOM/restart is likely and strands in_progress beads invisible to bd ready. Fix: checkpoint merged/blocked/failed sets to disk + stale-claim reaper on re-run.

title: decompose: inject a mandatory production floor (not opt-in per-feature)
description: Only app-skeleton is always poured. Auth/authz(openfga)/otel/audit/tenancy/IaC enter only if a vision must-have happens to map to them, and templates/vision.md asks for product features not infra. Example vision yields skeleton+crud+maybe-auth. Fix: mandatory production-floor formula set for any app declaring auth/data, with decompose fidelity check aware of it.

title: deploy: nothing is ever deployed (terraform plan-only, all apply steps human-gated)

No smoke test, health check, SLO, alerting, backup verification. User gets a git repo that passes tests, not a running app. Fix: unattended deploy-to-dev path (CI-issued creds, auto-created state backend) + post-deploy health/smoke bead.

title: workflows migration: skills to dynamic workflows
description: Convert compose/quality-pass/split skills into a single /decompose dynamic workflow, and convert build-batch into a /build-batch dynamic workflow. End state: one umbrella decompose command produces a blessed atomic bead DAG with adversarial fidelity cross-check vs plan.md; one build-batch workflow fans out builders and serializes the merger. Old skills deleted after new workflows ship and docs are updated. Human gate between /decompose and /build-batch preserved.

title: /decompose dynamic workflow (subsumes compose+quality-pass+split)
description: Umbrella workflow that takes plan.md and produces a blessed atomic bead DAG. Phases: parse plan -> fan-out pour beads per feature -> fan-out atomize oversized beads -> fan-out quality-score each epic -> adversarial fidelity cross-check (two agents independently compare DAG vs plan.md, both must agree) -> topological dep audit. Output: decomposeReport.md + BLESSED|NEEDS-FIX verdict. Replaces /compose, /quality-pass, /split.

title: A1: write workflows/decompose.spec.md
description: Author the design doc for the /decompose dynamic workflow. Structure mirrors workflows/retro.spec.md: header, how-this-workflow-runs (hand-authored JS pattern, NOT first-run-generated), inputs (--plan path, --since/--until, --no-file dry-run), phases (parse, pour, atomize, quality, fidelity-cross-check, dep-audit), per-phase agent specs (name, tools, inputs, outputs, failure), run-completion behavior, stopping conditions, do-not list. The spec.md is the contract; A2 implements it. Acceptance: workflows/decompose.spec.md exists; every phase has named agents with declared tools and IO; the fidelity cross-check phase explicitly describes the two-verifier-then-reconcile pattern with disagreement handling.

title: A2: write workflows/decompose.js
description: Hand-author the canonical JS script that implements workflows/decompose.spec.md. The script orchestrates subagents per the dynamic-workflow API (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows). All phases declared in the spec must be present: parse, pour (fan-out per feature), atomize (fan-out per oversized bead, loops until atomic), quality (fan-out per epic), fidelity-cross-check (2 verifiers per concern + reconciler), dep-audit (topo sort + cycle detect). Intermediate state lives in JS variables, not Claude's context. Acceptance: workflows/decompose.js exists; install.ps1 -DryRun shows it would be hardlinked to ~/.claude/workflows/; the script's phase structure matches the spec one-to-one.

title: A3: smoke test /decompose on a real plan.md
description: End-to-end run of /decompose against a real plan.md fixture (pick an existing sibling app with a plan.md, or fabricate a 5-feature plan.md in workflows/test/decompose-fixture.md). Verify: (1) pour phase creates the expected number of root beads; (2) atomize phase splits any bead with >6 ACs; (3) quality phase emits scores and remediations; (4) fidelity cross-check runs both verifiers and reconciles; (5) blessed verdict emitted iff all checks pass AND every plan feature has at least one bead. Capture transcript or /workflows progress-view output. Acceptance: smoke-test results recorded in workflows/test/decompose.smoketest.md OR in the bead notes; the run produced a non-empty decomposeReport.md; no phase errored.
notes: Smoke test requires user-initiated invocation of /decompose against a fresh app repo (decompose.spec.md Phase 1 refuses if the repo has open beads, so it cannot run against autonomous-build itself). I cannot dispatch /decompose from inside /build-next — slash commands are user-driven, and the workflow runtime is not exposed as a tool to subagents. SMBuild (../SMBuild/) is the natural smoke-test target: it has plan.md, plan.lock.json, vision.md, tenets.md, and no .beads/ directory. To complete this bead the human should: (1) cd ../SMBuild; (2) ./install.ps1 in autonomous-build first to hardlink workflows/decompose.js into ~/.claude/workflows/; (3) /decompose --no-file (dry run) to verify all 8 phases complete without mutating bd; (4) inspect decomposeReport.md; (5) if all phases ran and report is non-empty, write findings to autonomous-build/workflows/test/decompose.smoketest.md (transcript or summary of phase outcomes + verdict); (6) close this bead. If /decompose --no-file passes, also run /decompose live to verify the mutation path (Phase 2 bd init + jankurai adopt, Phase 3 pours actually happen).

title: A4: delete skills/compose, skills/quality-pass, skills/split
description: After /decompose is live and docs reflect it, delete the three subsumed skill directories. install.ps1's stale-junction cleanup will remove the corresponding ~/.claude/skills/ junctions on the next install run; no manual junction cleanup required. Acceptance: skills/compose/, skills/quality-pass/, skills/split/ no longer exist in the repo; running install.ps1 reports the stale junctions as cleaned; no remaining references to these skill paths in any repo file (verified by grep).
notes: Deleted skills/compose, skills/quality-pass, skills/split (commit on main). Verified by git grep: no live source references to these skill paths remain — only the dated retro (retros/retro-autonomous-build-2026-05-28.md, historical git-analysis record, must not be rewritten) and workflows/decompose.spec.md's migration-rationale notes (lines 10/399, which intentionally document the replacement and this deletion). install.ps1 stale-junction cleanup NOT run here: junctions are an NTFS/Windows concept and this session is Linux — defer to the user's next Windows ./install.ps1 run, which will drop the ~/.claude/skills/{compose,quality-pass,split} junctions automatically.

title: /build-batch dynamic workflow (convert from skill)
description: Convert skills/build-batch/SKILL.md from an in-conversation orchestration skill into a dynamic-workflow JS script. Phases: preflight (meta-guard + bd-healthy + main-clean + ready beads exist) -> build-and-merge loop (fan-out builders up to --workers, serialized merger with post-merge gate, filesTouched conflict filter) -> summary + auto-invoke /escalate if blocked. Hand-authored JS in workflows/build-batch.js, design rationale in workflows/build-batch.spec.md.

title: B1: write workflows/build-batch.spec.md
description: Author the design doc for the /build-batch dynamic workflow. Port the existing skills/build-batch/SKILL.md sections into spec.md shape (matching workflows/retro.spec.md structure). Preserve every semantic from the existing skill: meta-mode guard in preflight phase, --workers/--max-merges/--budget flags, filesTouched conflict filter, BUILD_COMPLETE marker file at .bd-build-complete.json, serialized merger with post-merge gate, in-memory pipeline state expressed as script variables, structured BATCH COMPLETE summary, auto-invoke /escalate on blocked, optional /retro on full drain. The spec is the design rationale; B2 implements it. Acceptance: workflows/build-batch.spec.md exists; every section from the old SKILL is represented; meta-mode guard documented as Phase 0; no semantics dropped without an explicit Removed: note.

title: B2: write workflows/build-batch.js
description: Hand-author the canonical JS script that implements workflows/build-batch.spec.md. Dynamic-workflow API per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows. Required behaviors: Phase 0 meta-guard agent refuses to proceed if skills/build-next/SKILL.md exists; Phase 1 preflight (bd healthy, main clean, ready beads); Phase 2 build-and-merge loop fans out builders up to --workers concurrent, polls .bd-build-complete.json markers, runs ONE merger agent at a time against main with post-merge gate, applies filesTouched conflict filter when selecting new candidates; Phase 3 summary + auto-invoke /escalate if blockedSet non-empty. Acceptance: workflows/build-batch.js exists; install.ps1 -DryRun shows hardlink target; script never spawns 2+ concurrent mergers; every bead state transition (claim/build/merge/close OR block OR fail) is reachable.

title: B3: smoke test /build-batch in an app repo
description: End-to-end run of /build-batch in a sibling app repo (NOT autonomous-build itself -- meta-mode guard refuses). Pick an app with at least 3 ready non-epic beads. Run /build-batch --workers 2 --max-merges 2 and verify: (1) preflight passes; (2) two builder agents dispatch concurrently in worktrees; (3) merger agent runs after each builder completion, never concurrently with another merger; (4) post-merge gate fires on main; (5) batch terminates cleanly with BATCH COMPLETE summary. Acceptance: smoke-test results in workflows/test/build-batch.smoketest.md OR in the bead notes; transcript shows >=1 successful builder->merger->close cycle; meta-mode guard verified separately by invoking in autonomous-build and confirming refusal.
notes: Smoke test requires user-initiated invocation of /build-batch in a sibling app repo with >=3 ready non-epic beads. I cannot dispatch /build-batch from inside /build-next — slash commands are user-driven, and Phase 0 of /build-batch correctly refuses to run in autonomous-build (skills/build-next/SKILL.md is the meta-mode marker). To complete this bead the human should: (1) ./install.ps1 in autonomous-build to hardlink workflows/build-batch.js into ~/.claude/workflows/; (2) cd to an app repo with >=3 ready non-epic beads (e.g. ../SMBuild after running /decompose to populate beads, or any app currently mid-build); (3) verify the meta-mode guard separately by first running /build-batch in autonomous-build itself and confirming it refuses with 'MODE: meta'; (4) in the app repo, run /build-batch --workers 2 --max-merges 2; (5) confirm: (a) Phase 1 pre-flight passes; (b) two beads-builder agents dispatch concurrently in worktrees; (c) merger runs sequentially (never two concurrent); (d) post-merge gate fires on main between merges; (e) batch terminates with BATCH COMPLETE summary listing merged/blocked/failed sets; (6) write findings to workflows/test/build-batch.smoketest.md (transcript summary + verdict); (7) close this bead. Note: v1 build-batch.js implements wave-dispatch rather than continuous polling — the smoke test validates this is acceptable for v1 or surfaces the need for true poll-loop semantics in a follow-up bead.

title: Validate converted formulas end-to-end with bd cook + bd mol pour
description: After formulas are converted to bd's TOML schema (sibling bead), prove they actually work end-to-end. Today the /compose skill assumes a working bd cook + bd mol pour pipeline but it has never been tested against the current bd build. WHAT TO DO: (1) in a scratch project (e.g. /tmp/bd-pour-test or a temp dir), bd init + copy the converted formulas in. (2) run bd cook app-skeleton --mode=runtime --var app_name=demo --var language=typescript --var framework=nextjs --var test_framework=vitest --var lint_tool=biome — verify the proto output is well-formed. (3) bd mol pour <proto-id> --parent <epic-id> — verify the spawned issues have correct titles, types, descriptions, and dependency edges (needs:). (4) repeat for crud-feature with a realistic entity. (5) bd ready should return the leaf tasks. (6) if anything fails or behaves unexpectedly, document the deviation in formulas/README.md and either fix the formula or escalate. Depends on the formula-conversion bead.

title: /compose: bd mol pour has no --parent flag; reparent via bd dep add after pour
description: /compose documents 'bd mol pour <proto-id> --parent <epic-id>' but bd 0.55.3's mol pour command has no --parent flag (verified during autonomous-build-s9f validation). Pouring spawns its own root epic; to put it under the app-level epic, reparent after pouring. WHAT TO DO: in skills/compose/SKILL.md Step 4, replace the two-step 'bd cook --persist + bd mol pour --parent' pattern with a one-step 'bd mol pour <formula-name> --var ...' followed by 'bd dep add <pour-root-id> <app-epic-id> --type parent-child'. Capture the pour-root-id from pour's output. Also: drop the unnecessary cook --persist intermediate — pour accepts formula names directly.

title: Add installer script that junctions autonomous-build/skills/* into ~/.claude/skills/
description: Currently every new skill in autonomous-build/skills/ requires a manual 'mklink /J ~/.claude/skills/<name> ...' step before Claude Code can invoke it as a slash command. Easy to forget; caught when /quality-pass (autonomous-build-74i) wasn't immediately invocable. Idempotent — re-running is safe. Document the invocation in README.md (probably 'Setup' section). Worth checking whether the same problem exists for autonomous-build/formulas/ → ~/.beads/formulas/ (it might already be junctioned but a future formula won't auto-link).

title: Convert formulas to bd canonical TOML schema
description: The autonomous-build formulas use a YAML schema that bd 0.55.3 does not recognize. bd cook expects .formula.json or .formula.toml. Canonical schema differs from current YAML in several ways: top-level field is 'formula' not 'name'; variables go in [vars.<name>] tables; step deps use 'needs:' not 'depends_on:'; bd has no separate 'acceptance:' field (must be embedded in description); top-level 'type = workflow' and 'version = 1' are required. WHAT TO DO: (1) rewrite formulas/crud-feature.formula.yaml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.yaml as .formula.toml files matching the canonical bd schema (use ~/Documents/Github/beads/.beads/formulas/beads-release.formula.toml as reference). (2) decide whether to embed the acceptance criteria inside each step's description as a clear 'Acceptance:' block, or to extend bd. (3) delete the .yaml files after conversion. (4) update formulas/README.md to describe the actual TOML schema, removing the speculative YAML schema sketch. Discovered 2026-05-28 while trying to add testPlan fields per autonomous-build-a2m.

title: vision: stop asking human tech questions; default to Jankurai stack; agent-consult off-stack
description:
During /vision plan phase, do NOT ask the user any technical/stack questions. Always default to the canonical Jankurai stack:

- Rust core
- TypeScript + React + Vite product surface
- PostgreSQL as truth
- Generated contracts (TS<->Rust types)
- Exception-only Python for AI/data service (never for repo tooling, product truth, or general backend glue)

For technical decisions outside this stack (e.g. a formula gap, a novel integration choice), spawn a parallel team of 3 agents (architect / reviewer / counter-arguer), synthesize their consensus, and record the decision in plan.md as 'Decided by agent consult'. Never surface tech as an open question for the human.

The 'Open questions for human' section in plan.md is reserved for *product/scope* ambiguities only (contradictory must-haves, missing success metric, undefined non-goals).

Acceptance:
- skills/vision/SKILL.md updated: process step 'Resolve every no-preference with a recommendation' replaced with 'Default to Jankurai stack always; never ask tech questions; agent-consult for off-stack decisions'
- A docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md exists with the canonical stack written out and referenced from the vision skill
- templates/vision.md §7 (Tech preferences) removed or replaced with a note that tech is auto-resolved
- Stopping conditions in vision skill no longer block on tech ambiguity; only product/scope
close_reason: Implemented: vision skill no longer asks tech questions. title: /build-next: skip epics in bd ready output
description: bd ready returns epics (which are containers, not work). /build-next would claim an epic, fail to implement it, and waste a tick. bd ready has no --type filter, so the skill has to filter client-side. WHAT TO DO: in skills/build-next/SKILL.md Step 1, change the bd ready call to fetch the full ready list and pick the first non-epic. PowerShell sketch: bd ready --json | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { $_.issue_type -ne 'epic' } | Select-Object -First 1. Empty-result handling stays the same. Caught during the first attempt to run /build-next on autonomous-build's own meta-work, 2026-05-28.

title: Add tenets system: docs/TENETS.md + templates/tenets.md + /vision integration
description: Introduce a workflow-level tenets document (autonomous-build/docs/TENETS.md) that codifies the principles the loop falls back on for build-time judgment calls (escalate over guess, gate is the contract, atomic bead, scope discipline, reversibility bias, formula precedence, failure visibility, idempotency, meta-vs-app, lock-vs-narrative). Make /vision produce a per-app tenets.md alongside plan.md + plan.lock.json that inherits the workflow tenets and adds app-specific tenets derived from vision.md non-goals/constraints + plan.lock agentConsults + deferred openQuestions. Goal: when an agent hits a judgment call during build-next, it has a concrete document to consult.
close_reason: Workflow-level docs/TENETS.md created (10 tenets + source-of-truth ordering + hard-conflict resolutions + non-tenets list). Per-app templates/tenets.md created that inherits T1-T10 and derives A-tenets from vision.md non-goals/constraints + plan.lock agentConsults + deferred openQuestions. skills/vision/SKILL.md updated: new step 9 derives tenets, Outputs section now lists tenets.md alongside plan.md + plan.lock.json, Do-not section forbids asking the user about tenets and forbids paraphrasing T1-T10. README.md pipeline diagram + repo layout updated; CLAUDE.md Orientation updated. Follow-up filed as autonomous-build-2g0 for /build-next consumption.

title: decompose cross-dep wiring reports success but under-applies edges
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: the Phase 3 cross-dep wiring agent reported applied=24, but the Phase 6 fidelity verifier found only 13 cross-feature edges present, with 9 missing between successfully-poured molecule epics (e.g. grammar to tenant-boot, contracts to customer, customer to property/job). The agent claimed success without confirming edges landed; a likely cause is wiring against pourRoot IDs versus the molecule-epic IDs the verifier checks. Verification: compared the wiring agent log (applied 24) against decomposeReport.md coverage section (13 applied, 15 missing).
acceptance_criteria: workflows/decompose.js Phase 3 cross-dep wiring re-queries each edge via bd dep or bd show after bd dep add and reports the verified-present count, not the attempted count; the dep audit reconciles declared vs present edges between molecule epics.
close_reason: Phase 3 cross-dep wiring agent now re-queries each edge after bd dep add (bd show --json / bd dep show), retries once against visible molecule-epic IDs on a pourRoot-vs-epic mismatch, and reports verifiedPresent (not attempted) plus a missing[] list via a new schema. Spec Phase 3 + Phase 7 updated.
